title: Measure folded reducer with internal producer-side source
motivation: The standalone folded reducer boundary sweep shows ppc4 and some higher-utilization ppc2 points fail during physical implementation. The failure pattern is consistent with top-level pin-site pressure from exposing all producer-side partial fragments as macro IO rather than an arithmetic datapath limit.
hypothesis: If the producer-side partial-fragment bus is flattened into the block as internal wiring, ppc4 and high-utilization ppc2 points should become physically feasible more often than the standalone wrapper. The resulting metrics should be interpreted as a producer-coupled macro proxy, not as a replacement for the standalone reducer interface contract.

expected_behavior.functional: The underlying folded reducer RTL is unchanged and continues exact grouped reduction over fixed partial groups.
expected_behavior.timing_model: Reducer latency remains partials / partials_per_cycle accepted chunks; the wrapper adds a deterministic internal source counter only for physical pin-pressure isolation.
expected_behavior.physical_model: Producer-side fragment pins are internal nets, while consumer-side reduced outputs and counters remain top-level macro pins.

risks: The deterministic internal source adds small counter and fanout cost, so area/power include a producer-stub overhead.
This does not prove the final producer network schedule; it only separates arithmetic feasibility from macro-boundary pin pressure.
